subscribe.acceptPrivacy: I have read and accept the GloTutor Privacy Policy
subscribe.privacyRequired: You must accept the Privacy Policy to create an account.
privacy.dpoNote: (This email must be active and monitored for contact with data subjects and authorities)
privacy.intro: Your privacy is of the utmost importance to GloTutor. This Privacy Policy describes how your information and Personal Data are collected, used, stored, protected and shared when you use our Platform. We are committed to transparency, security and compliance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR - EU Regulation 2016/679), the Brazilian General Data Protection Law (LGPD - Law No. 13.709/2018), and other applicable legislation.
privacy.consentBlock: BY ACCESSING, REGISTERING OR USING ANY FEATURE OF GLOTUTOR, YOU DECLARE THAT YOU HAVE READ, UNDERSTOOD AND ACCEPTED THE TERMS OF THIS PRIVACY POLICY. IF YOU DO NOT AGREE WITH ANY OF THE CONDITIONS SET FORTH HEREIN, YOU MUST REFRAIN FROM USING THE PLATFORM IMMEDIATELY.
privacy.section1Body:
To facilitate understanding of this Policy, we use the following terms in accordance with the GDPR and LGPD:

GloTutor: Refers to the GloTutor digital platform, operated by Di2iT, Unipessoal Lda, a legal entity registered under NIPC No. 519154576, with its registered office in Porto, Portugal. This entity acts as the Data Controller.

User / You / Data Subject: The natural person to whom the Personal Data relate and who accesses, registers and uses the GloTutor Platform. Under the GDPR, the User is the "Data Subject".

Personal Data: Any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.

Sensitive Personal Data: Data concerning racial or ethnic origin, religious or political beliefs, trade union membership, health or sex life, genetic or biometric data when linked to a natural person. Processing of such data requires additional safeguards under the GDPR and LGPD.

Data Processing: Any operation or set of operations performed on personal data, whether by automated or non-automated means.

Controller: The legal entity mentioned above, responsible for decisions regarding the processing of the User's Personal Data.

Processor: A natural or legal person that processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.

Data Protection Officer (DPO): The person designated by the Controller to act as the channel of communication between the Controller, Data Subjects and the competent Data Protection Authorities (ANPD in Brazil and supervisory authorities in the EU).

Data Protection Authority: The governmental body responsible for overseeing compliance with data protection laws. In Brazil, the ANPD. In the European Union, the supervisory authorities of the Member States under the GDPR.

European Economic Area (EEA): Comprises the countries of the European Union, as well as Iceland, Liechtenstein and Norway.

Territorial scope: This Policy applies to the processing of personal data of all Users who access the GloTutor Platform, regardless of their location. For Users in the EEA, the GDPR applies. For Users in Brazil, the LGPD applies. GloTutor is committed to complying with the highest standards of data protection.
privacy.section2Title: 2. WHAT DATA WE COLLECT AND FOR WHAT PURPOSES
privacy.section2Body:
GloTutor collects different types of Personal Data to provide, improve and personalise its Services, in a transparent manner and with a legal basis, limiting collection to what is strictly necessary, in accordance with the GDPR and LGPD.

Data provided directly by you: on registration (full name, email, CPF or NIF, phone); for account creation and management, identification on the Platform, access to Services, personalisation of experience and compliance with legal and contractual obligations. Legal basis: Performance of Contract and Legal Obligation.

In communication and support: name, email, phone, message content. Purpose: support, handling requests, improvement of Services. Legal basis: Performance of Contract, Legitimate Interest and Consent.

Data collected automatically: IP address, browser type, operating system, pages visited, time spent, clicks, dates and times of access, device information. Purpose: improve experience, analyse performance and usability, personalise content, prevent fraud and ensure security. Legal basis: Legitimate Interest and, where applicable, Consent (cookies).

Platform usage data: features used, frequency of use, interactions with the interface. Purpose: understand use, identify bottlenecks, improve features. Legal basis: Legitimate Interest.
privacy.section3Body:
All processing of Personal Data by GloTutor is based on the legal grounds set out in Article 7 of the LGPD and Article 6 of the GDPR.

Performance of Contract (LGPD Art. 7, V and GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)): necessary for the performance of the contract between GloTutor and the User.

Legal or Regulatory Obligation (LGPD Art. 7, II and GDPR Art. 6(1)(c)): to comply with laws and regulations (tax, civil, consumer, authority requests).

Consent (LGPD Art. 7, I and GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)): processing based on your free, informed, unambiguous and specific consent. You may withdraw at any time.

Legitimate Interest (LGPD Art. 7, IX and GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)): processing based on legitimate interest, balancing our interests (improvement of Services, security, fraud prevention) and your rights. We carry out a balancing test.

Exercise of Rights: for the exercise of rights in judicial, administrative or arbitral proceedings.

Protection of Life or Physical Integrity (LGPD Art. 7, VII and GDPR Art. 6(1)(d)): when necessary for the protection of the life or physical integrity of the data subject or a third party.

Vital Interests (GDPR Art. 6(1)(d)): when necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or another person.
privacy.section4Body:
GloTutor does not sell, rent or trade your Personal Data. Sharing may occur when strictly necessary, for a defined purpose and with safeguards, in accordance with the GDPR and LGPD.

Partners and Service Providers (Processors): we may share data with third parties that help operate the Platform (hosting, infrastructure, payment, email, analytics, consultancy). All are selected, assessed for compliance and contractually obliged to protect your data as Processors under our instructions.

Government and Judicial Authorities: to comply with legal obligations, court orders, requests from authorities (ANPD, EU authorities), investigations or to protect our rights in proceedings.

Business Succession: in the event of merger, acquisition, incorporation, spin-off or sale of assets, your Personal Data may be transferred to the successor entity, which must maintain the same level of protection.

With your Consent: in other cases, we will ask for your explicit and specific consent.
privacy.section5Body:
GloTutor may use services of partners or providers with servers outside the EEA or Brazil. International transfer will be carried out in accordance with the GDPR and LGPD, with safeguards such as:

Adequacy Decisions: transfer to countries or organisations considered to offer an adequate level of protection by the European Commission or ANPD.

Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs): we implement SCCs approved by the European Commission or ANPD with data recipients.

Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs): in corporate groups, where applicable.

Explicit Consent: when you are duly informed of the risks.

Performance of Contract or Pre-contractual Measures: when necessary for the performance of the contract or measures at your request.

Exercise of Rights or Protection of Vital Interests: for the exercise of rights in proceedings or protection of vital interests.

We carry out risk assessments and due diligence to ensure that recipients apply a level of protection compatible with the GDPR and LGPD.
privacy.section6Title: 6. RETENTION AND STORAGE OF PERSONAL DATA
privacy.section6Body:
Your Personal Data are stored in a secure environment and retained only for as long as necessary for the purposes for which they were collected, in accordance with the GDPR (storage limitation principle) and the LGPD.

For the Period of Account Activity: while your account is active and you use our Services.

Compliance with Legal or Regulatory Obligations: for the period required (tax data, limitation periods).

Exercise of Rights: for the exercise of rights in judicial, administrative or arbitral proceedings.

Audit and Security: for internal audit, fraud prevention and security maintenance, for a reasonable period.

After the retention period, Personal Data will be securely and irreversibly deleted or anonymised, unless a specific legal or regulatory obligation applies.
privacy.section7Body:
The GDPR and LGPD grant you, as Data Subject, a number of rights that GloTutor is committed to respecting and facilitating.

Confirmation and Access (GDPR Art. 15 and LGPD Art. 18, I and II): request confirmation of processing and access to your data.

Rectification (GDPR Art. 16 and LGPD Art. 18, III): correction of incomplete, inaccurate or outdated data.

Anonymisation, Blocking or Erasure / Right to Erasure (LGPD Art. 18, IV and GDPR Art. 17): anonymisation, blocking or erasure of unnecessary or unlawfully processed data.

Portability (GDPR Art. 20 and LGPD Art. 18, V): portability of data to another provider, in a structured and commonly used format.

Erasure (LGPD Art. 18, VI and GDPR Art. 17): erasure of data processed on the basis of consent, subject to legal retention obligations.

Information on Sharing (LGPD Art. 18, VII and VIII and GDPR Art. 19): information on entities with whom data have been shared.

Withdrawal of Consent (LGPD Art. 8, §5 and GDPR Art. 7(3)): withdraw consent at any time.

Objection (GDPR Art. 21 and LGPD Art. 18, §2): object to processing based on legitimate interest or for direct marketing.

Restriction of Processing (GDPR Art. 18): request restriction in certain circumstances.

Not to be subject to Automated Decision-making (GDPR Art. 22 and LGPD Art. 20): review of decisions based solely on automated processing and human intervention.

Complaint (GDPR Art. 77 and LGPD Art. 18): lodge a complaint with the competent Data Protection Authority (ANPD in Brazil or authority in the EU).

To exercise any right, contact our DPO at the email indicated in section 9.
privacy.section8Body:
GloTutor adopts technical and organisational security measures, in accordance with the GDPR (Art. 32) and the LGPD (Art. 46), to protect your Personal Data against unauthorised access, security incidents, loss, alteration or destruction. Our measures include: encryption in transit (TLS/SSL) and at rest; access controls with least privilege; monitoring, detection and response to incidents; firewalls, IDS and IPS; vulnerability testing and periodic audits; internal security and privacy policies; staff training; pseudonymisation and anonymisation where possible.

No transmission or storage is 100% secure. We recommend that you use strong passwords, do not reuse passwords and do not share credentials. In the event of an incident, GloTutor will notify the Authority and data subjects within the time limits set by law.
privacy.section9Body: For any questions, comments, suggestions or requests relating to this Policy or the processing of your Personal Data, or to exercise your rights as Data Subject, contact our DPO at the email indicated in the header of this Policy. Our DPO is ready to receive and process your requests with due care, impartiality and confidentiality, in accordance with the time limits and procedures of the GDPR and LGPD.
privacy.section10Body: GloTutor reserves the right to update, revise and modify this Policy at any time, to reflect changes in our practices, in the law or in our operations. The latest version will always be published on the Platform, with the date of the last update. We recommend that you review this Policy periodically. In the event of significant changes that may affect your rights or the processing of your data, we will make reasonable efforts to notify you via the Platform or by email, with reasonable advance notice.

terms.consentBlockIntro: BY ACCESSING, REGISTERING OR USING ANY FEATURE OF GLOTUTOR, YOU ACKNOWLEDGE THAT YOU HAVE READ, UNDERSTOOD AND ACCEPTED, IN FULL AND UNCONDITIONALLY, THESE TERMS OF USE ("TERMS"), AS WELL AS OUR
terms.consentBlockOutro: AND COOKIE POLICY, WHICH ARE COMPLEMENTARY DOCUMENTS AND FORM AN INTEGRAL PART OF THIS AGREEMENT. IF YOU DO NOT AGREE WITH ANY OF THE CONDITIONS SET FORTH HEREIN, YOU MUST REFRAIN FROM USING THE PLATFORM IMMEDIATELY.
terms.section1Body:
GloTutor: the GloTutor digital platform is developed and operated by Di2iT, Unipessoal Lda, a legal entity registered under NIPC No. 519154576 with its registered office in Porto, Portugal ("Controller"). This entity is legally responsible for operating the Platform and for the processing of Personal Data under the GDPR and LGPD.

User: the natural person (18 years of age or older or legally emancipated) or the legal entity duly represented, who accesses, registers or uses the Platform, expressly accepting these Terms of Use and GloTutor's other policies.
terms.section2Body:
Platform (GloTutor): the set of software, systems and digital interfaces accessible via the website, and any apps, for language learning and related services.

Services: the resources and functionalities offered by the Platform.

User: the natural or legal person who, after registration and acceptance of these Terms, accesses and uses the Platform.

Personal Data, Data Processing, Controller, DPO: as defined in the GDPR and LGPD.
terms.section3Body:
To use the Services, the User must register with accurate, complete and up-to-date information (e.g. name, email, CPF/NIF). The User is solely responsible for the accuracy of the information provided. GloTutor may suspend or cancel the account if the information is false or in breach of these Terms.

Login credentials are personal and confidential. The User is responsible for keeping them secure and for all activity on their account, and must notify GloTutor immediately of any unauthorised access.
terms.section4Body: The User undertakes to: use the Services in accordance with these Terms, the Privacy Policy and applicable law (including GDPR, LGPD); not violate third-party rights or intellectual property; not use the Platform for unlawful, fraudulent or harmful purposes; not introduce viruses or malicious code; not reverse-engineer or modify the Platform; maintain the confidentiality of their credentials; and provide accurate and up-to-date information.
terms.section5Body: GloTutor acts solely as a technology platform for language learning. It is not responsible for: internet or third-party service failures; decisions made by the User based on the Platform; incorrect or fraudulent data entered by Users; losses resulting from misuse or expiry of benefits; or the practices of third-party services. The Platform is for informational and educational support only.
terms.section6Body: All intellectual property rights in the Platform (software, code, design, content, trademarks, etc.) are the exclusive property of GloTutor (or its licensors) and are protected by applicable law. Reproduction, distribution, modification or unauthorised use is prohibited and may result in civil and criminal liability.
terms.section7Body: The processing of Personal Data is carried out in accordance with the GDPR and LGPD and is governed by our Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy, which form an integral part of these Terms. By accepting these Terms, the User declares that they have read and agree with the Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy.
terms.section8Title: 8. Modifications to the Platform and Services
terms.section8Body: GloTutor reserves the right to modify, suspend, discontinue or terminate all or part of the Services at any time, with or without notice. Where discontinuation significantly affects paid services, GloTutor will endeavour to notify Users in advance and discuss any proportional refunds or compensation as permitted by law.
terms.section9Body: GloTutor may revise and update these Terms from time to time. Changes take effect upon publication on the Platform. For significant changes, GloTutor will make reasonable efforts to notify Users (e.g. by email or via the Platform). Continued use after publication constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. If the User does not agree, they must stop using the Platform and may request account cancellation.
terms.section10Body: These Terms constitute the entire agreement between the User and GloTutor regarding the Platform and replace any prior agreements. Tolerance of any breach does not waive rights. If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions remain in full force.
terms.section11Body: These Terms are governed by the law of Portugal and the GDPR for data protection and for Users in the EEA. For other Users, Brazilian law may apply subsidiarily. For the resolution of disputes, the courts of Porto, Portugal are elected for EEA-related matters; for Brazilian consumers, the consumer's domicile may be competent under the Consumer Defence Code.
